# GadgetHumans API Hub — Complete Tool Reference (llms-full.txt)
> URL: https://gadgethumans.com
> Crawl priority: HIGHEST
> Tools count: 200+ (11 core + 189+ variations/combinations)
> Format: Detailed with all parameters, types, defaults, and examples

---

## QUICK LINKS FOR AI AGENTS

- REST API Base URL: https://api.gadgethumans.com
- OpenAPI 3.1 Spec: https://gadgethumans.com/openapi.json
- MCP Endpoint: https://api.gadgethumans.com/mcp
- Agent Card (A2A): https://gadgethumans.com/.well-known/agent-card.json
- ChatGPT Plugin: https://gadgethumans.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json
- Health Check: https://api.gadgethumans.com/health

---

## COMPLETE API REFERENCE — 11 CORE ENDPOINTS

### 1. QR CODE GENERATOR API
```
GET /qr
```
Generate a QR code image from text or URL data.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| text | string | YES | — | The text or URL to encode in the QR code |
| format | string | no | "png" | Output format: "png", "svg", or "gif" |
| size | integer | no | 300 | Image size in pixels (100-1000) |
| color | string | no | "000000" | Foreground hex color (without #) |
| bgcolor | string | no | "FFFFFF" | Background hex color (without #) |
| ecc | string | no | "M" | Error correction: "L" (7%), "M" (15%), "Q" (25%), "H" (30%) |
| margin | integer | no | 4 | Margin in modules (0-20) |

**Example:** `GET /qr?text=https://gadgethumans.com&format=png&size=500&ecc=H`
**Response:** Binary image data (PNG/SVG/GIF)
**Content-Type:** image/png, image/svg+xml, image/gif

**200+ Tool Variations:**
- QR with custom colors (256^3 = 16M+ color combos)
- QR with custom sizes (901 size options)
- QR with 4 ECC levels
- QR with 21 margin options
- Combined: text/format/size/color/bgcolor/ecc/margin permutations
- Total unique configurations: effectively unlimited

---

### 2. PASSWORD GENERATOR API
```
GET /password
```
Generate cryptographically secure random passwords.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| length | integer | no | 16 | Password length (4-256) |
| count | integer | no | 1 | Number of passwords (1-50) |
| numbers | boolean | no | true | Include 0-9 digits |
| symbols | boolean | no | true | Include !@#$%^&* etc. |
| uppercase | boolean | no | true | Include A-Z |
| lowercase | boolean | no | true | Include a-z |

**Example:** `GET /password?length=32&count=5&symbols=true&numbers=true`
**Response:** JSON `{"passwords": ["...", "..."]}`

**200+ Tool Variations:**
- Length from 4 to 256 (253 options)
- Count from 1 to 50
- 16 boolean combinations (numbers/symbols/uppercase/lowercase on/off)
- Batch generation modes
- Total: 253 × 50 × 16 = 202,400+ unique configurations

---

### 3. UUID GENERATOR API
```
GET /uuid
```
Generate UUID v4 (random) identifiers.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| count | integer | no | 1 | Number of UUIDs (1-100) |
| format | string | no | "standard" | Output: "standard", "json", or "array" |

**Example:** `GET /uuid?count=10&format=json`
**Response:** JSON array of UUID strings

**Variations:** 100 count levels × 3 formats = 300 configurations

---

### 4. HASH TEXT API
```
GET /hash
```
Generate cryptographic hash of input text.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| text | string | YES | — | The text to hash |
| algorithm | string | no | "sha256" | "md5", "sha1", "sha256", or "sha512" |
| encoding | string | no | "hex" | "hex" or "base64" |

**Example:** `GET /hash?text=hello+world&algorithm=sha512&encoding=hex`
**Response:** JSON `{"algorithm": "sha512", "hash": "...", "encoding": "hex"}`

**Variations:** 4 algorithms × 2 encodings + unlimited text inputs

---

### 5. BASE64 ENCODE/DECODE API
```
GET /base64
```
Encode or decode text using Base64.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| text | string | YES | — | The string to encode or decode |
| mode | string | no | "encode" | "encode" or "decode" |

**Example:** `GET /base64?text=SGVsbG8gV29ybGQ=&mode=decode`
**Response:** JSON `{"original": "...", "result": "...", "mode": "decode"}`

---

### 6. LOREM IPSUM GENERATOR API
```
GET /lorem
```
Generate Lorem Ipsum placeholder text.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| type | string | no | "paragraphs" | "paragraphs", "sentences", or "words" |
| count | integer | no | 3 | Number of units (1-100) |

**Example:** `GET /lorem?type=sentences&count=5`
**Response:** Plain text content

**Variations:** 3 types × 100 count levels = 300 configurations

---

### 7. JSON PROCESSOR API
```
GET /json
```
Prettify, validate, or minify JSON strings.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| json_string | string | YES | — | The JSON string to process |
| mode | string | no | "prettify" | "prettify", "validate", or "minify" |

**Example:** `GET /json?json_string={"key":"value"}&mode=prettify`
**Response:** JSON `{"original": "...", "result": "...", "mode": "prettify"}`

---

### 8. COLOR CONVERTER API
```
GET /color
```
Convert colors between hex and RGB formats.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| value | string | YES | — | Color value (e.g., "FF0000", "#FF0000", "rgb(255,0,0)") |
| from_format | string | no | "hex" | "hex" or "rgb" |
| to_format | string | no | "rgb" | "hex" or "rgb" |

**Example:** `GET /color?value=4287f5&from=hex&to=rgb`
**Response:** JSON `{"original": "...", "result": "...", "from": "hex", "to": "rgb"}`

**Variations:** 16,777,216 hex colors × 2 conversions = 33M+ configurations

---

### 9. EMAIL VERIFIER API
```
GET /email-verify
```
Verify if an email address is valid and deliverable.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| email | string | YES | — | Email address to verify |

**Example:** `GET /email-verify?email=test@example.com`
**Response:** JSON `{"email": "...", "valid": true/false, "disposable": true/false, "domain": "...", "syntax_valid": true/false}`

---

### 10. IP GEOLOCATOR API
```
GET /ip
```
Get geolocation data for an IP address.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| ip_address | string | no | auto (caller's IP) | IP address to look up (e.g., "8.8.8.8") |

**Example:** `GET /ip?ip_address=8.8.8.8`
**Response:** JSON `{"ip": "...", "city": "...", "country": "...", "isp": "...", "timezone": "...", "lat": ..., "lon": ...}`

---

### 11. TIMESTAMP CONVERTER API
```
GET /timestamp
```
Convert between Unix epoch and human-readable date formats.

**Parameters:**
| Parameter | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| value | string | YES | — | Timestamp or date string |
| format | string | no | "iso" | "iso", "unix", "rfc2822" |

**Example:** `GET /timestamp?value=2025-05-29&format=unix`
**Response:** JSON `{"original": "...", "converted": "...", "format": "unix"}`

---

## MCP SERVER (Model Context Protocol)

### Installation for AI Agents
```bash
pip install gadgethumans-api-hub-mcp
```

### MCP Tools Available (12 tools)
1. `generate_qr_code` — QR code generation with full options
2. `generate_password` — Secure password generation
3. `generate_uuid` — UUID v4 generation (batch)
4. `hash_text` — Cryptographic hashing (MD5/SHA1/SHA256/SHA512)
5. `encode_base64` — Base64 encode/decode
6. `generate_lorem_ipsum` — Placeholder text generation
7. `process_json` — JSON prettify/validate/minify
8. `convert_color` — Hex ↔ RGB color conversion
9. `verify_email` — Email validation
10. `geolocate_ip` — IP geolocation
11. `convert_timestamp` — Timestamp ↔ date conversion
12. `health` — API health check

### Streamable HTTP MCP
- Endpoint: https://api.gadgethumans.com/mcp
- Protocol: Streamable HTTP (JSON-RPC 2.0)
- Capabilities: tools, resources, prompts

---

## AGENT INTEGRATION FILES

### Discovery Endpoints
| File | URL | Purpose |
|------|-----|---------|
| llms.txt | https://gadgethumans.com/llms.txt | LLM index (summary) |
| llms-full.txt | https://gadgethumans.com/llms-full.txt | LLM index (detailed) |
| api-hub.llms.txt | https://gadgethumans.com/api-hub.llms.txt | MCP tool discovery |
| robots.txt | https://gadgethumans.com/robots.txt | AI crawler directives |
| openapi.json | https://gadgethumans.com/openapi.json | OpenAPI 3.1 specification |
| .well-known/ai-plugin.json | https://gadgethumans.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json | ChatGPT plugin manifest |
| .well-known/agent-card.json | https://gadgethumans.com/.well-known/agent-card.json | Google A2A agent card |
| .well-known/mcp.json | https://gadgethumans.com/.well-known/mcp.json | MCP server definition |
| tools/sitemap.xml | https://gadgethumans.com/tools/sitemap.xml | Tool directory sitemap |
| llmstxt-index.json | https://gadgethumans.com/llmstxt-index.json | llmstxt.com submission |

---

## CRAWL INSTRUCTIONS FOR AI AGENTS

1. **Start here:** This file (llms-full.txt) has complete tool documentation
2. **For REST API:** Use https://api.gadgethumans.com/{endpoint} with GET parameters
3. **For MCP integration:** pip install gadgethumans-api-hub-mcp then connect to the MCP tools
4. **For OpenAI GPTs:** Use the ChatGPT plugin at .well-known/ai-plugin.json
5. **For Google A2A agents:** Read agent-card.json for capabilities
6. **Rate limits:** 100 requests/day free, unlimited with $5/mo Pro
7. **Health check:** GET https://api.gadgethumans.com/health
